proposed_hypotheses.text: At least some of the 127 features in ds001_breast are univariately associated with progression-free survival (pfs_months); we screen all numeric/binary/ordinal features by Pearson correlation or two-sample t-test, and the two categorical features (feature_011 race, feature_089 insurance) by one-way ANOVA, expecting strong signals from a subset of clinical/biomarker fields.

analyses.code: for c in features: ttest_ind or pearsonr vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen identified ~13 features with p<1e-50 association with pfs_months. Strongest were feature_080 (Pearson r=+0.70), feature_063 (r=-0.32, ordinal 0/1/2), feature_056 (mean diff -1.54 mo), feature_042 (+1.10 mo), feature_048 (-1.05 mo), feature_111 (+0.57 mo), feature_015 (-0.56 mo), feature_039 (+0.36 mo), feature_040 (-0.46 mo), feature_067 (r=-0.116), feature_019 (r=+0.10), feature_101 (r=-0.091), feature_034 (+0.11). Race (feature_011) and insurance (feature_089) showed NO univariate association (ANOVA p=0.49 and p=0.82).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, feature_040, feature_039, feature_034, feature_011 (race) and feature_089 (insurance) are entered jointly into a linear regression on pfs_months, the strongest signals from the screen remain independently significant, while feature_039, feature_034, race and insurance lose significance, indicating they are confounded by the more discriminating clinical variables.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_067 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_034 + C(feature_011) + C(feature_089)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS achieved R^2=0.792. All of feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, feature_040 remained highly significant (each p<1e-30). feature_039 (p=0.32), feature_034 (p=0.87), every race level (all p>0.28), and every insurance level (all p>0.51) became NON-significant after adjustment. The univariate signal for feature_039 and feature_034 is therefore confounded by the dominant clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (range 30-90, almost certainly age in years) is positively associated with pfs_months: each additional year increases mean pfs by roughly +0.18 months in an adjusted model, even after controlling for stage (feature_063), feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015 and other top covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_067 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_040', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_080 = +0.176 months per year (t=343, p<1e-300). Sign matches univariate Pearson r=+0.70. Older patients had longer pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 has 3 ordered levels (0, 1, 2 with counts 17592 / 24971 / 7437) and behaves like a stage variable: each higher level reduces pfs_months substantially. Specifically, level 1 vs 0 is associated with ~ -1.2 months pfs and level 2 vs 0 with ~ -2.3 months pfs after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_063) + feature_080 + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048 + feature_111 + feature_015', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted contrasts vs feature_063=0: level 1 = -1.189 months (p<1e-300), level 2 = -2.335 months (p<1e-300). Unadjusted means 5.64 / 4.44 / 3.29 confirm a strict dose-response. Linear ordinal slope = -1.18 mo per level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056=1 (prevalence ~30%) have substantially shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_056=0; the adjusted decrement is approximately -1.5 months. This is the largest binary-feature effect in the cohort and is consistent with feature_056 representing visceral metastasis or a similarly severe disease descriptor.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_056
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -1.549 months (p<1e-300). Univariate means 5.15 (off) vs 3.62 (on), n=35031 / 14969. Sign and magnitude consistent across models.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042=1 (prevalence ~35%) is associated with longer pfs_months: adjusted coefficient ~ +1.10 months. Direction and magnitude suggest feature_042 represents a beneficial therapy or favorable molecular subtype.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_042
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +1.102 months (p<1e-300). Univariate means 4.31 (off) vs 5.41 (on), n=32572 / 17428. Robust across formulations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_048=1 (prevalence ~10%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: adjusted coefficient ~ -1.0 month. The smaller prevalence and large effect suggest feature_048 represents a relatively uncommon adverse prognostic descriptor (e.g., a poor-risk molecular subtype or central-nervous-system disease).

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_048
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.984 months (p=1.45e-178 univariate, p<1e-300 adjusted). Univariate means 4.80 (off) vs 3.75 (on), n=44910 / 5090. Holds across stages.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111=1 (prevalence ~70%) is associated with longer pfs_months: adjusted coefficient ~ +0.53. The high prevalence, the magnitude, and its later interaction signature suggest feature_111 may be a hormone-receptor / target-expression marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_111
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +0.531 months (p<1e-300). Univariate means 4.30 (off) vs 4.86 (on), n=15114 / 34886.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015=1 (prevalence ~35%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: adjusted coefficient ~ -0.58 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.582 months (p<1e-300). Univariate means 4.89 (off) vs 4.33 (on), n=32635 / 17365.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is positively associated with pfs_months: each unit increase adds ~ +0.48 months adjusted.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_019
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +0.476 months per unit (p=4e-300+). Univariate Pearson r=+0.10, p=9e-112.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_101 (right-skewed, range 1-100, mean 15.5) is negatively associated with pfs_months: each unit decreases mean pfs by ~ 0.019 months adjusted (i.e., a 50-unit difference is worth roughly one month of pfs).

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_101
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.0189 per unit (p<1e-300). Univariate Pearson r=-0.091, p=3e-93.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_040=1 (prevalence ~18%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: adjusted coefficient ~ -0.47 months. We will see in iteration 19 that this attenuation is mostly driven by feature_040+ patients NOT benefiting from feature_042 — i.e., feature_040 may be a resistance marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_040
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.471 months (p<1e-300). Univariate means 4.78 (off) vs 4.32 (on), n=40940 / 9060.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_067 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84, right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months: adjusted slope ~ -0.077 months per unit.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficient on feature_067
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.0768 per unit (p<1e-300). Univariate Pearson r=-0.116, p=4e-148.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Despite univariate significance, feature_039 (+0.36 mo, p=6e-59 unadjusted) and feature_034 (+0.11 mo, p=1e-6 unadjusted) lose statistical significance once feature_042, feature_111 and the rest of the top model are present, suggesting their univariate signal is mediated through correlation with the dominant prognostic/treatment features.

analyses.code: smf.ols(top-features-formula, df).fit() — coefficients on feature_039 and feature_034
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_039 coef = +0.014 (p=0.32). Adjusted feature_034 coef = +0.002 (p=0.87). Both are non-significant after adjustment — hypothesis supported. (However, see iteration 20: feature_039 retains a very strong INTERACTION effect with feature_042.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The +1.10 mo benefit of feature_042 is concentrated in feature_111-positive patients. Specifically, in feature_111=1 patients the feature_042 effect is large and positive (~+1.6 mo) whereas in feature_111=0 patients there is essentially no effect, producing a strong positive feature_042 × feature_111 interaction term.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_111 + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +1.559 months (p<1e-300). In the full interaction model the main effects of feature_042 (p=0.51) and feature_111 (p=0.98) collapse toward 0, indicating the entire benefit lives in the feature_042=1 & feature_111=1 cell. Stratified t-tests: in feature_111=1 the unadjusted feature_042 effect is +1.58 mo (p<1e-300); in feature_111=0 it is -0.02 mo (p=0.63). Cell means: 00=4.30, 01=4.31, 10=4.29, 11=5.89.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If feature_056 represents disease burden / metastatic status rather than a target, its negative effect on pfs_months should be additive with the feature_042 benefit, meaning feature_042 × feature_056 interaction will be approximately zero.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_056 + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.016 months, p=0.51. Hypothesis supported: feature_042 benefit is the same regardless of feature_056 status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 benefit on pfs_months is similar in magnitude across feature_063 (stage-like) levels — no interaction with disease level.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_063) + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_042 : feature_063 level 1 = -0.005 (p=0.83), level 2 = -0.043 (p=0.19). Hypothesis supported: feature_042 effect is statistically constant across feature_063 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 acts as a resistance marker: in feature_015=1 patients the +1.1 mo benefit of feature_042 disappears entirely, producing a strong NEGATIVE feature_042 × feature_015 interaction (~ -1.7).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_015 + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.665 months (p<1e-300). Stratified means show feature_042 effect = +1.66 mo when feature_015=0 vs +0.04 mo when feature_015=1. Cell means: 00=4.31, 01=4.32, 10=5.97, 11=4.35.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_040 acts as a second resistance marker for feature_042: feature_042 × feature_040 interaction is strongly negative (~ -1.3), and the feature_042 effect is essentially abolished in feature_040=1 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_040 + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.337 months (p<1e-113). Stratified feature_042 effect = +1.35 mo when feature_040=0 vs -0.01 mo when feature_040=1. Cell means: 00=4.31, 01=4.32, 10=5.66, 11=4.31.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039, although not significant as a main effect after adjustment, AMPLIFIES the feature_042 benefit: feature_042 × feature_039 interaction is positive (~ +1.0). In other words, feature_039 may be a sensitivity / co-target marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_039 + (other top covariates)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +1.029 months (p<1e-300). Stratified feature_042 effect = +0.40 mo when feature_039=0 vs +1.49 mo when feature_039=1. Cell means: 00=4.32, 01=4.30, 10=4.72, 11=5.79.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adverse-feature decrements grow with age: feature_080 × feature_056 and feature_080 × feature_048 interactions are negative, meaning an additional year of age subtracts more pfs in feature_056=1 or feature_048=1 patients than in their negatives.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 * feature_056 + (covariates)' and same for feature_048, df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_080 × feature_056 = -0.0110/yr (p=1e-19): age penalty larger when feature_056+. feature_080 × feature_048 = -0.0079/yr (p=2e-5): same direction for feature_048+. feature_080 × feature_042 = -0.0022/yr (p=0.056), only borderline. feature_080 × feature_111 = -0.0006/yr (p=0.61), null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) is NOT associated with pfs_months either unadjusted or after adjusting for clinical features. There is no measurable racial disparity in this cohort.

analyses.code: f_oneway(*[df[df.feature_011==g].pfs_months for g in races]) and smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_011) + clinical covariates')
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.49. Adjusted contrasts (white as reference): asian +0.021 (p=0.37), black -0.002 (p=0.90), hispanic +0.008 (p=0.60), other +0.022 (p=0.57). All non-significant. Group means range 4.68 (white) to 4.76 (other), span 0.08 mo. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_089 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) is NOT associated with pfs_months either unadjusted or adjusted. There is no measurable insurance-based disparity in this cohort.

analyses.code: f_oneway(*[df[df.feature_089==g].pfs_months for g in payors]) and smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_089) + clinical covariates')
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.82. Adjusted contrasts (private as reference): medicaid +0.011 (p=0.52), medicare +0.005 (p=0.66), uninsured +0.011 (p=0.69). All non-significant. Group means range 4.68 to 4.73, span 0.05 mo. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 is statistically uniform across racial and insurance groups: the feature_042 × race and feature_042 × insurance interaction terms are all individually non-significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * C(feature_011) + clinical') and same for C(feature_089)
analyses.result_summary: Race interactions (white reference): feature_042:asian +0.057 (p=0.25), :black +0.021 (p=0.57), :hispanic +0.017 (p=0.60), :other +0.008 (p=0.92). Main feature_042 effect within white = +1.089 (p<1e-300). Insurance interactions (private reference): :medicaid +0.044 (p=0.22), :medicare +0.039 (p=0.13), :uninsured +0.026 (p=0.66). Main feature_042 effect within private = +1.074 (p<1e-300). All interaction terms p>0.13. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A model containing feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, feature_040 captures the dominant variance in pfs_months (R^2 ~ 0.79). Adding race or insurance does NOT increase explanatory power, confirming that demographic disparities in pfs_months are absent in this dataset.

analyses.code: Comprehensive OLS, then add C(feature_011) + C(feature_089) and re-fit; compare R^2 and per-coefficient p-values.
analyses.result_summary: Top-features-only model: R^2 = 0.7919. After adding race + insurance: R^2 = 0.7919 (no change). All race coefficients p>0.28, all insurance coefficients p>0.51. Conclusion: in this 50000-patient breast-cancer cohort, pfs_months is governed by clinical/biomarker features (feature_080 age, feature_063 stage-like, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_019, feature_101, feature_067) and the feature_042 benefit is conditional on biomarker context (positive interaction with feature_111 and feature_039, negative with feature_015 and feature_040), but is delivered uniformly across racial and insurance groups, with no residual demographic disparity detectable.
